title: Vaccinium gaultheriifolium
intro: Vaccinium gaultheriifolium, also known as Gaultheria, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium gaultheriifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Vaccinium gaultheriifolium include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and colds.
